instructions.title: How to Use Baseball Stats Tracker
instructions.gs1: Create or Select a Team — From the Dashboard, tap + New Team. If you already have teams, you'll see a list of your existing teams to quickly switch between them. Tap any team to go to its page, or tap + Create New Team to add a new one with a team name and level (Little League, Travel, Recreational, High School, or College).
instructions.gs2: Add Players — Open your team page and tap + Add Player. Enter each player's first name, last name, jersey number, batting hand (Right, Left, or Switch), throwing hand, and primary position.
instructions.gs3: Edit Players — Need to make a change? Tap the Edit button next to any player in the roster to update their name, jersey number, position, or handedness.
instructions.gs4: Create a Season — Tap + Season on your team page to add a season (e.g., “Spring 2026”). Seasons let you group games and filter stats over time. To remove a season entered incorrectly, tap the red Delete link next to it in the Seasons list on your team page. Games in that season are preserved.
instructions.gs5: Schedule a Game — Tap + New Game, enter the opponent name, game date, time, location, whether you're home or away, and the number of innings. Optionally assign the game to a season. The home/away selection affects the default batting order — as the home team, your team bats in the bottom of the inning; as the away team, you bat in the top.
instructions.preGameIntro: Before first pitch, open your game and get everything ready:
instructions.pg1: Set Your Lineup — Tap Set Lineup on the live scoring page. Add players from your roster to the batting order, assign each a defensive position for this game, and use the up/down arrows to reorder. Save when you're ready.
instructions.pg2: Add Opponent Pitchers — When your team is batting, use the pitcher dropdown and tap + New to add the starting pitcher's name and throwing hand (Left or Right). You can add relief pitchers as the game progresses.
instructions.pg3: Add Opponent Batters — Switch to Opponent Batting mode and tap + Add Batter to add opposing batters with their name, jersey number, and batting hand. You can add these as each batter comes up. If you've played this opponent before, their roster is automatically loaded from the previous game, so you won't need to re-enter them.
instructions.liveIntro: The Live Scoring page is the primary screen you use at the field. Open any game and you're ready to go.
instructions.scoreboardText: The scoreboard at the top shows the current score, inning (Top or Bottom), and outs count. Everything updates automatically as you record at-bats.
instructions.modeToggleText: Use the toggle at the top to switch between Our Team Batting (blue) and Opponent Batting (red). This determines whose at-bats you are recording and ensures the score updates for the correct team.
instructions.ro1: The current batter is shown automatically based on your lineup order.
instructions.ro2: Select the opposing pitcher from the dropdown (or add a new one if a pitching change occurs).
instructions.ro3: Choose the at-bat result from the buttons below (see At-Bat Results section).
instructions.ro4: Adjust RBI count and mark if the runner scored using the +/- buttons.
instructions.ro5: Record stolen bases or caught stealing if applicable.
instructions.ro6: For batted balls, tap the spray chart diamond to record where the ball was hit.
instructions.recordingOursNote: The app automatically tracks outs, advances innings (3 outs ends the half-inning), and updates the score.
instructions.rop1: Switch to Opponent Batting mode using the toggle.
instructions.rop2: The current opponent batter is shown automatically (or add a new batter).
instructions.rop3: Optionally track individual pitches using the pitch tracking panel (see Pitch Tracking section below).
instructions.rop4: Select the at-bat result, set RBI/runs scored, and tap the spray chart for batted balls.
instructions.selectBatterText: Need to record an at-bat for someone out of order? Tap any player in the batting order list to select them. Tap again or use “Back to next up” to return to the automatic rotation.
instructions.autoInningText: Three outs automatically end the half-inning. The app switches from Top to Bottom (or Bottom to Top with an inning increment). Outs reset to zero for the new half-inning. Double plays count as 2 outs.
instructions.realTimeSyncText: The live scoring page automatically refreshes every 3 seconds. This means multiple people can follow the same game from different devices — one person scores at the field while coaches or parents see updates in real time on their own phone or computer.
instructions.pitchTrackingIntro: When in Opponent Batting mode, use the pitch tracking panel to record each pitch before logging the at-bat result. This tracks your pitcher's performance and builds a pitch-by-pitch record.
instructions.pt1: Tap the strike zone diagram to mark where the pitch was located.
instructions.pt3: Tap + Add Pitch to log the pitch. It appears as a numbered circle on the strike zone.
instructions.pt4: The ball-strike count updates automatically. Foul balls do not add a strike when already at 2 strikes.
instructions.pt5: Use Undo to remove the last pitch if you make a mistake.
instructions.pt6: Once the pitch sequence is complete, record the at-bat result as usual.
instructions.pitchTrackingNote: All pitches are saved with the at-bat and visible in the game log, including the total pitch count for each at-bat.
instructions.atBatResultsIntro: Select one of these outcomes for each at-bat. Each button shows the abbreviation with the full name displayed below it for quick reference:
instructions.resultE: E — Error (batter reaches on a fielding error)
instructions.atBatNote: Note: Walks (BB, IBB), Hit By Pitch (HBP), Sacrifice Fly (SF), Sacrifice Bunt (SAC), and Catcher's Interference (CI) are not counted as official at-bats but are counted as plate appearances.
instructions.atBatExtrasIntro: In addition to the at-bat result, you can record the following for each plate appearance:
instructions.extraRBI: RBI — Use the +/- buttons to set how many runs batted in on this at-bat (0 or more).
instructions.extraScored: Runner Scored — Toggle Yes/No to indicate if the batter scored a run (e.g., on a home run or subsequent play).
instructions.extraSB: Stolen Bases — Use the +/- buttons if the batter stole any bases during this at-bat.
instructions.extraCS: Caught Stealing — Use the +/- buttons if the batter was caught stealing.
instructions.extraHitLoc: Hit Location — For any batted ball (hit or out in play), tap the spray chart diamond to mark where the ball was hit or fielded.
instructions.editingPrevText: Need to add a stolen base or caught stealing after you've already moved to the next batter? Tap any entry in the Game Log to expand it. Use the SB and CS +/- buttons to update the values, then tap Save. This is useful when a runner steals a base or gets picked off while the next batter is at the plate.
instructions.oppPitchersText: When your team is batting, select the opposing pitcher from the dropdown or tap + New to add one. Enter their name and throwing hand (Left or Right). Each of your team's at-bats is linked to the opposing pitcher, which enables pitcher-specific stat splits later in Stats Search. Add new pitchers as relief pitchers enter the game.
instructions.oppBattersText: Switch to Opponent Batting mode and tap + Add Batter to create opponent batter entries with name, jersey number (optional), and batting hand (Right, Left, or Switch). The app auto-rotates through opponent batters just like your lineup, or you can tap to manually select a batter.
instructions.yourPitchingText: When in Opponent Batting mode, the app tracks which of your team's pitchers is on the mound. This records pitching appearances including outs recorded, hits allowed, runs, earned runs, walks, strikeouts, home runs allowed, pitch count, hit batters, and wild pitches.
instructions.sprayChartsText: When recording any batted-ball result (hit or out in play), a baseball diamond diagram appears. Tap where the ball landed or was fielded to record the location. The location is saved with the at-bat.
instructions.viewingSprayIntro: You can view spray charts in three ways:
instructions.viewSpray1: During Live Scoring — Mark hit locations as you record at-bats.
instructions.viewSpray2: Game Spray Chart — From any game, view all batted-ball locations from that single game.
instructions.viewSpray3: Team Spray Chart — From your team page, view cumulative spray chart data across all games. Filter by individual player or view the whole team at once.
instructions.boxScoreIntro: Every game has a box score that you can view during or after the game. The box score includes:
instructions.boxScore1: Inning-by-Inning Scoreboard — A traditional baseball scoreboard showing runs scored in each inning, with columns for total Runs (R), Hits (H), and Errors (E). The away team is on top, home team on bottom, and the current inning is highlighted in blue.
instructions.boxScore2: Batting Stats Table — Each player in your lineup with AB, H, R (runs scored), RBI, BB, K (strikeouts), SB, and AVG for that game.
instructions.boxScore3: Play-by-Play Log — Chronological list of every at-bat showing the batter, result, RBI, stolen bases, and inning marker (e.g., T1, B3).
instructions.seasonStatsIntro: From your team page, tap Season Stats to see cumulative batting statistics for every player across all games.
instructions.seasonStatsColIntro: The stats table includes these columns (tap a column header to sort):
instructions.seasonStatsRecord: The page also shows your team's overall record (Wins - Losses - Ties) at the top.
instructions.advSearchIntro: The Stats Search page lets you filter and analyze your players' performance with powerful filters:
instructions.advSearch1: Team — Select which of your teams to analyze.
instructions.advSearch2: Opponent — Filter by opponent team name (partial match).
instructions.advSearch3: Pitcher — Filter by opposing pitcher name (partial match).
instructions.advSearch4: Pitcher Hand — See how your players hit against lefties vs. righties.
instructions.advSearch5: Date Range — Filter by start and end dates.
instructions.advSearchNote: Results show each player's stats (G, AB, H, HR, RBI, BB, K, SB, AVG, OBP, OPS) for only the at-bats matching your filters. For example, you can see how your team hits against a specific pitcher or how they perform against left-handed pitching.
instructions.paText: Total times a batter comes to the plate, including walks, HBP, sacrifices, and all other outcomes.
instructions.abText: Plate appearances minus walks, HBP, sacrifice flies, sacrifice bunts, and catcher's interference. This is the denominator for batting average.
instructions.avgText: Hits ÷ Official At-Bats. Walks, HBP, sacrifices, and catcher's interference are not counted as official at-bats.
instructions.obpText: (Hits + Walks + HBP + CI) ÷ (At-Bats + Walks + HBP + Sacrifice Flies + CI). Measures how often a batter reaches base safely.
instructions.slgText: Total Bases ÷ Official At-Bats. Singles = 1, Doubles = 2, Triples = 3, Home Runs = 4.
instructions.opsText: OBP + SLG. A combined measure of a hitter's ability to get on base and hit for power.
instructions.sbPctText: Stolen Bases ÷ (Stolen Bases + Caught Stealing). Shows baserunning efficiency.
instructions.mgmtUndo: Undo Last — Removes the most recent at-bat and reverts the score, outs, and inning as needed. Use this to fix mistakes.
instructions.mgmtEndGame: End Game — Marks the game as final. The final score is locked and the game appears with a green “Final” badge in your games list.
instructions.mgmtBoxScore: Box Score — View a traditional box score with batting stats and play-by-play log at any time during or after the game.
instructions.mgmtSprayChart: Game Spray Chart — View all batted-ball locations from the game on a color-coded diamond diagram.
instructions.mgmtStatus: Game Status — Games show as “Scheduled” before any at-bats, “In Progress” with the current inning during the game, and “Final” after you end the game.
instructions.mgmtDeleteAll: Delete All Games — From your team's page, scroll below the games list to find the red Delete All Games button. This permanently removes all games and their stats for the team. A confirmation dialog will appear before anything is deleted. This is useful when starting a fresh season or cleaning up test data.
instructions.step1: Create the game from your team page with opponent name, date, and home/away.
instructions.step2: Set your lineup — add players and assign positions.
instructions.step3: Top of 1st (Opponent Batting) — Switch to Opponent Batting mode. Add opponent batters as they come up. Optionally track pitches for each at-bat. Record each result. After 3 outs, the inning flips automatically.
instructions.step4: Bottom of 1st (Your Team Batting) — Switch to Our Team Batting. Add the opposing pitcher. Record each at-bat result with RBIs, runs scored, and hit location. After 3 outs, inning advances to Top of 2nd.
instructions.step5: Continue alternating between Opponent Batting and Our Team Batting each half-inning. Add new opponent pitchers or batters as they enter the game.
instructions.step6: End the game when the final out is recorded. Tap End Game to mark it as final.
instructions.step7: Review the box score and spray chart after the game!
instructions.quickDarkMode: Dark Mode — Toggle between light and dark themes using the sun/moon icon in the toolbar. Dark mode is great for outdoor games with bright sunlight.
instructions.quickSignOut: Sign Out — Click “Sign Out” in the navigation bar to log out from your account.
instructions.tip1: Set up your roster and lineup before the game starts so you can focus on scoring during play.
instructions.tip2: Add opponent pitchers as they enter the game so each at-bat is linked to the correct pitcher for splits analysis.
instructions.tip3: Use the spray chart tap on every batted ball — even outs. Over a season, this reveals valuable hitting tendencies.
instructions.tip4: Record pitch locations during opponent at-bats to analyze your pitcher's tendencies and strike zone command.
instructions.tip5: Check the Stats Search page regularly to see how your team performs against different opponents and pitcher types.
instructions.tip6: Use the Undo Last button right away if you record something incorrectly — it's easier to fix in the moment.
instructions.tip7: Have a second person help — one person watches the game, the other records on the app. With real-time sync, both devices stay up to date automatically.
instructions.tip8: When facing a repeat opponent, their batter roster loads automatically — saving you time on game day setup.
instructions.tip9: Use dark mode at outdoor games for better screen visibility in bright sunlight.
instructions.tip10: Check the box score during the game to see the traditional inning-by-inning scoreboard with R/H/E columns, just like a real ballpark.

landing.subtitle: The Baseball & Softball Stats Tracker App for Youth, Little League, and Travel Ball Teams
landing.hook1: Your kid had the game of his life on Saturday.
landing.hook2: Can you remember what he did at the plate in the 4th?
landing.hookBody: Neither can he. Every season, thousands of at-bats and breakout moments disappear into a paper scorebook nobody opens again. Baseball Stats Tracker keeps every at-bat — forever — from your phone in the bleachers.
landing.ctaSub: One-time payment. Score your first game in 60 seconds.
landing.featureLiveDesc: Score games in real-time from your phone at the field. Track every at-bat, stolen base, and pitching change.
landing.featureStatsDesc: Automatic calculation of AVG, OBP, SLG, OPS, and more. Filter stats by opponent, pitcher, and handedness.
landing.featureSprayDesc: See where your players hit the ball. Per-game and season-long spray charts with color-coded hit types.
landing.storyH2: I was the dad in the bleachers with a Sharpie and a clipboard.
landing.story1: I was trying to keep score one-handed while answering “Daddy, did you see that?” between innings. I'd come home with smudged paper, no spray chart, no OBP, and no clue how my son was actually trending.
landing.story2: I tried the “pro” apps. $19/month. $30/month. Built for college coaches with three monitors and an assistant. My wife took one look and said, “You're not going to use that.”
landing.story4: So I built the thing I actually wanted: tap-to-score from the bleachers, automatic stats the second the inning ends, spray charts that show you exactly where your kid is putting the ball, and a live link grandma can open from her couch in Florida.
landing.story6: Because the kids playing right now? They're only 11 once.
landing.offerSub: Built for the bleachers, not the press box. Big buttons. One thumb. Sun-readable.
landing.cmpGet1: Live game scoring from your phone
landing.cmpRep1: Paper scorebook + a pen that died in the 3rd
landing.cmpGet3: Pitching stats: IP, ERA, K, BB, pitch count
landing.cmpGet5: Splits vs. lefties, righties, specific opponents
landing.cmpGet6: Live link grandparents can follow from anywhere
landing.mathH3: The math, if you're a numbers person:
landing.mathBody: By the end of one season, you've already saved enough for new cleats. By the end of three, you've saved enough for a bat your kid will actually be excited about.
landing.seoH2: The Best Baseball & Softball Stats App for Youth Teams
landing.seoBody: Whether you coach Little League, travel ball, rec league, high school, or college — score every game live from your phone and get professional-quality statistics automatically. No spreadsheets. No paper scorebooks. Record at-bats in real time and the app calculates batting average (AVG), on-base percentage (OBP), slugging percentage (SLG), OPS, RBIs, stolen bases, and every stat that matters — instantly. See the full <featuresLink>feature list</featuresLink> or jump straight to <pricingLink>pricing</pricingLink>.
landing.cardLiveDesc: Score baseball and softball games in real time from any phone or tablet. Multiple devices sync every 3 seconds — so coaches, parents, and fans can all follow along live.
landing.cardBattingDesc: AVG, OBP, SLG, OPS, RBIs, stolen bases, plus full pitching: IP, ERA, K, BB, HR, pitch count, wild pitches. Calculated automatically as you score.
landing.cardSprayDesc: See where each player hits the ball. Color-coded by hit type. Per-game and season-long views reveal exactly where your hitters live.
landing.cardSplitsDesc: Filter by opponent, pitcher, pitcher handedness, and date range. See how your players hit against lefties, righties, or that one team you always face in the playoffs.
landing.levelsH2: Built for Every Level of Baseball & Softball
landing.levelLLDesc: Track your player's development without complicated scorekeeping software.
landing.levelTravelDesc: Reliable stats for tournaments and showcases. Multiple seasons and teams in one place.
landing.levelHSDesc: Pro-level stat tracking at a fraction of the cost of other scorekeeping apps.
landing.faqQ1: What stats does Baseball Stats Tracker track?
landing.faqA1: Batting: AVG, OBP, SLG, OPS, AB, H, 2B, 3B, HR, RBI, BB, K, SB, CS, SF, SAC. Pitching: IP, ER, K, BB, H, HR allowed, pitch count, HBP, wild pitches.
landing.faqA2: Yes. Works perfectly for both baseball and softball at any level.
landing.faqA3: $39 one-time. No monthly fees, no subscriptions, no in-app purchases. Pay once, use it forever with unlimited teams, seasons, and games.
landing.faqQ4: Can multiple people follow a game live?
landing.faqA4: Yes. One person scores at the field while coaches, parents, and fans follow in real time from their own device. Syncs every 3 seconds.
landing.faqQ5: What devices does it work on?
landing.faqA5: Any device with a browser — iPhone, Android, iPad, laptop, desktop. No app store download. Just open baseballstatstracker.com.
landing.faqQ6: How is this different from other baseball stats apps?
landing.faqA6: Most charge $10–$30/month. Baseball Stats Tracker is a flat $39 one-time purchase. You get live scoring, spray charts, advanced splits, opponent tracking, and real-time multi-device sync — features that typically cost $200+/year elsewhere. Read our <learnLink>honest comparison of GameChanger alternatives</learnLink> for the full breakdown.
landing.urgencyEyebrow: The season is happening with or without the stats
landing.urgencyH2: Every weekend you wait, another game disappears.
landing.urgencyBullet1: — Another double down the line your kid will forget.
landing.urgencyBullet2: — Another strike-three-looking moment that could've taught him something.
landing.urgencyBullet3: — Another season where, when the high school coach asks “What's his OBP against lefties?” — you have to shrug.
landing.urgencyClose: Your kid is putting in the reps. The least you can do is keep the receipts.
landing.closeBullet2: Nothing to cancel — you just own it
landing.closeBullet3: Try free first — pay only when you're ready to score games.
landing.ctaSub2: No subscription. No upsells. No “Pro tier.”

common.footerRights: © {year} Baseball Stats Tracker. All rights reserved.

features.h1: Everything You Need to Track Baseball & Softball Stats
features.intro: Baseball Stats Tracker gives coaches and parents professional stat tracking tools at a fraction of the cost. One app handles live scoring, batting stats, pitching stats, spray charts, box scores, and opponent scouting — for both baseball and softball.
features.liveH2: Live Game Scoring from Your Phone
features.liveIntro: Score baseball and softball games in real time from any phone, tablet, or computer. The live scoring interface is designed for speed — tap the at-bat result, adjust RBIs, and you're done. The app automatically tracks outs, advances innings, and updates the running score.
features.liveBullet1: Real-time sync — Multiple devices stay in sync every 3 seconds. One person scores while coaches and parents follow along live.
features.liveBullet2: Automatic inning tracking — Three outs automatically end the half-inning. No manual inning changes needed.
features.liveBullet3: Full at-bat results — Singles, doubles, triples, home runs, walks, HBP, strikeouts, groundouts, flyouts, lineouts, popouts, fielder's choice, double plays, sacrifice flies, sacrifice bunts, errors, and catcher's interference.
features.liveBullet4: Stolen bases & caught stealing — Track baserunning stats on every plate appearance.
features.liveBullet5: Undo support — Made a mistake? Undo the last at-bat instantly.
features.battingIntro: Every stat is calculated automatically as you score. No formulas, no spreadsheets, no math. Just score the game and your stats are ready.
features.battingFormula: Stats use standard MLB formulas. OBP = (H + BB + HBP + CI) / (AB + BB + HBP + SF + CI). Sacrifice flies and bunts are not counted as official at-bats. All calculations match what you see on MLB.com and ESPN.
features.pitchingP1: Track your pitchers' performance with detailed stats: innings pitched, outs recorded, hits allowed, runs, earned runs, strikeouts, walks, home runs allowed, pitch count, hit batters, and wild pitches. Use the pitch-by-pitch tracking tool to record individual pitch locations on a strike zone diagram.
features.pitchingP2: Pitch types include called strikes, swinging strikes, foul balls, balls, in play, and hit by pitch — each color-coded on the strike zone. Track pitch counts per at-bat and per game to manage your pitchers effectively.
features.sprayIntro: Tap the baseball diamond during live scoring to record where each ball was hit. Over a season, spray charts reveal hitting patterns that help coaches make lineup decisions, adjust defensive positioning, and work on hitting weaknesses.
features.sprayBullet1: Per-game spray charts — See all hit locations from a single game.
features.sprayBullet2: Season spray charts — Cumulative data across all games, filterable by player.
features.sprayBullet3: Color-coded hit types — Singles (green), doubles (blue), triples (orange), home runs (red), outs (gray).
features.boxP: Every game gets a professional inning-by-inning box score showing runs, hits, and errors — just like you see at a Major League ballpark. The box score includes complete batting stat lines for every player (AB, H, R, RBI, BB, K, SB, AVG) and a chronological play-by-play game log.
features.searchIntro: Go beyond basic stats with powerful filtering. Search your team's stats by opponent, pitcher name, pitcher handedness (lefty vs. righty), and date range. Answer questions like:
features.searchQ1: How does my team hit against left-handed pitching?
features.searchQ2: What are our stats against a specific rival team?
features.searchQ3: How has our batting average trended over the season?
features.searchQ4: Who hits best against a particular pitcher?
features.searchOutro: This kind of splits analysis was previously only available to professional and college teams. Now your Little League or travel ball team can use the same data-driven approach.
features.oppP: Track opponent pitchers and batters throughout the game. Link every at-bat to the opposing pitcher for later splits analysis. When you face a repeat opponent, their entire roster loads automatically from the previous matchup — saving valuable setup time on game day.
features.pricingP: No monthly fees. No subscriptions. No per-team charges. No hidden costs.
features.pricingUnlimited: Unlimited teams, seasons, and games — forever.
features.pricingSub: Create your account free. Purchase when ready to score games.
features.cmpH2: Why Coaches Choose Baseball Stats Tracker

pricing.intro: No monthly fees. No per-team charges. No feature gates. Pay once, use forever.
pricing.feat3: Live game scoring from any device
pricing.feat4: Automatic batting stats (AVG, OBP, SLG, OPS, and more)
pricing.securePayment: Secure payment via PayPal. Your account is activated within 24 hours of purchase.
pricing.compareIntro: Most baseball stats apps charge $10-$30 per month. Over a single season, that's $60-$180. Over three years, you'd spend $360-$1,080. Baseball Stats Tracker is just $39 total — forever.
pricing.faqA1: You can create a free account and explore the app. You'll be able to set up teams, add players, and view the interface. Creating and scoring games requires the $39 purchase.
pricing.faqA2: No. $39 is the complete price. No monthly fees, no per-team charges, no feature upgrades, no in-app purchases. Every feature is included.
pricing.faqA3: Yes. Your purchase includes unlimited teams, seasons, and games. Coach three teams across two age groups? No problem — one account covers everything.
pricing.faqA4: Payment is processed securely through PayPal. After payment, your account is activated within 24 hours (usually much faster). No credit card information is stored on our servers.
pricing.bottomSub: Explore the app free. Purchase when you're ready.
pricing.faqQ5: What devices does it work on?
pricing.faqA5: Any device with a browser — iPhone, Android, iPad, laptop, or desktop. No app store download required. Just open baseballstatstracker.com from any browser and you are ready to score.
pricing.faqQ6: Can I try it before I buy?
pricing.faqA6: Yes. Create a free account and explore the full app — set up teams, add players, and navigate every screen. Scoring and saving games requires the one-time $39 purchase.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: Last updated: June 2026. Plain-English summary of how Baseball Stats Tracker handles your data.
privacy.collectP: When you create an account we collect your email address and name. When you score games we store the data you enter — players, at-bats, stats, and scores — in your account. We also collect basic usage data (pages visited, errors) through Google Analytics to help us improve the app.
privacy.useP: We use your email to identify your account and send account-related messages such as payment confirmation. We use your game data to display your stats and sync across your devices. We do not use your data for advertising and we do not sell it to anyone.
privacy.shareP: We use the following services to run the app: Firebase (authentication), Supabase (database), Vercel (hosting), PayPal (payments), and Google Analytics (usage analytics). Each service processes only the data necessary to function. We do not share your personal data with any other third parties.
privacy.retainP: Your account data is kept for as long as your account is active. If you want your data deleted, email us and we will remove your account and all associated game data within 7 days.
privacy.securityP: All data is transmitted over HTTPS. Passwords are never stored — authentication is handled by Firebase. Game data is stored in a private database scoped to your account only. No other user can access your data.
privacy.kidsP: Baseball Stats Tracker is designed for coaches and parents, not for children to use directly. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children under 13. Player names and stats entered are at the discretion of the parent or coach who owns the account.

learn.title: GameChanger Alternatives in 2026: 5 Honest Options for Youth Baseball Parents (And the $39 One We Built)
learn.whyH2: The honest version of why you're here
learn.whyP1: You opened GameChanger this season and saw the bill. Or your league switched to a paid tier. Or you just spent fifteen minutes trying to figure out where the spray chart lives, and your kid is up in two minutes.
learn.whyP2: You're not alone. “GameChanger alternatives” is one of the most-searched youth-baseball queries every spring, and the reasons are always some combination of three things:
learn.whyLi1: Price. GameChanger's pricing has crept up over the years, especially for travel ball families with multiple kids on multiple teams.
learn.whyLi2: Complexity. It's built for a 13-year-old with two thumbs and a press-box coach with three monitors. It's not built for a dad in the bleachers trying to score one-handed while holding a juice box.
learn.whyP3: If any of those is why you're reading this — keep reading. I'm going to compare five real options I've actually used, including the $39 one I built (and I'll tell you when it's not the right fit).
learn.wantH2: What you actually want in an alternative
learn.wantP: Before we get to the table, here's what matters when you're picking a stats app as a parent or volunteer coach:
learn.wantLi1: One-handed scoring from your phone. You're standing. Your kid is up. Big buttons. Sun-readable.
learn.wantLi2: Stats that don't need a manual. AVG, OBP, OPS, RBIs — auto-calculated. You shouldn't need a Sabermetrics degree.
learn.wantLi3: Live sync to grandma's phone. So she can follow from Florida.
learn.wantLi4: Spray charts — actually useful for coaching positioning and seeing where your kid's hits are landing.
learn.wantLi5: Works on whatever phone you already have. No app store install if possible. iPhone, Android, iPad — just a browser.
learn.wantLi6: A price that doesn't make you flinch in April.
learn.tableNote: Pricing as of writing; check current vendor sites before deciding.
learn.s1H3: 1. Baseball Stats Tracker — the one I built ($39, one-time)
learn.s1Pitch: I was the dad in the bleachers with a Sharpie and a clipboard. I tried the “pro” apps. My wife took one look and said, “You're not going to use that.” She was right. So I built the thing I actually wanted.
learn.s1Well1: Tap-to-score from the bleachers. Big buttons, designed for one thumb.
learn.s1Well2: Auto stats: AVG, OBP, SLG, OPS, RBI, SB, K, BB — calculated as you score.
learn.s1Well3: Pitching stats: IP, ERA, K, BB, pitch count.
learn.s1Well4: Spray charts per game and per season.
learn.s1Well5: Live link grandparents can open from any browser.
learn.s1Well6: Splits vs. lefties, righties, specific opponents.
learn.s1Well7: $39 once. No subscription. Pay once, use it forever.
learn.s1Well8: Works for baseball and softball, every level — Little League through high school.
learn.s1Well9: Free account to explore before you buy — no credit card required.
learn.s1ShortLabel: Where it's not the right fit:
learn.s1Short1: If you're a college-level coach who needs custom advanced metrics beyond what I track, you may want something heavier.
learn.s1Short2: If you need deep video integration (clipping at-bats to film), look elsewhere — I don't do that yet.
learn.s1Short3: If your league mandates GameChanger for game data submission, you're stuck.
learn.s1Links: <featuresLink>See the full feature list</featuresLink> or <pricingLink>jump to pricing</pricingLink>.
learn.s2Pitch: iScore has been around forever and is favored by some high school and travel coaches who want a lot of knobs. It can do almost everything.
learn.s2Well1: Genuinely deep stat tracking with lots of config.
learn.s2Well3: Coaches who want to nerd out can.
learn.s2ShortLabel: Where it falls short for parents:
learn.s2Short1: The UI shows its age — lots of menus, lots of taps to do one thing.
learn.s2Short2: Subscription pricing per team adds up if you have multiple kids.
learn.s2Short3: Steeper learning curve than most parents have time for.
learn.s2Verdict: Great if you're a coach who lives in the app. Overkill for most parents.
learn.s3Pitch: GameChanger is the 800-pound gorilla. Most travel organizations use it. The interface is polished. The streaming feature is the best in the category.
learn.s3Well2: Best-in-class live streaming and broadcasting features.
learn.s3Well3: Wide adoption — if your team uses it, you know everyone else can follow along.
learn.s3Short1: Pricing has climbed over the years — what used to feel like a no-brainer now stings.
learn.s3Short2: Some features that were free are now behind paywalls.
learn.s3Short3: Built for organizations first; parents are along for the ride.
learn.s3Verdict: If your league mandates it, you don't have a choice. If they don't, the price is the issue.
learn.s4Pitch: This is how it was done for 100 years. It still works.
learn.s4Well1: $8 for a scorebook lasts a season.
learn.s4Well2: No batteries, no logins, no sync issues.
learn.s4Short1: You're scoring one-handed and trying to remember if that was a 6-4-3 or a 6-3.
learn.s4Short2: Stats? You're typing every at-bat into a spreadsheet on Sunday night. Or you're not.
learn.s4Short3: Spray charts? Hand-drawn on a half-sheet of paper.
learn.s4Short4: No live updates for the family.
learn.s4Verdict: Honest, romantic, and unsustainable past about three games into the season.
learn.s5Pitch: Not a stats app, but worth mentioning because some families combine a Pocket Radar with a scorebook or another app.
learn.s5Well2: Useful for older pitchers who care about velo.
learn.s5Short2: You still need a separate stats app on top of it.
learn.s5Verdict: Velocity is a small slice of the picture. Get a stats app first; add velo hardware if it's important to your kid's recruiting.
learn.verdictP: If you're a parent or volunteer coach reading this, you basically have three real choices:
learn.verdictLi1: You want the simplest thing that works, you don't want a subscription, and you're scoring your own kid's games: Try Baseball Stats Tracker. It's $39 once, and I built it for exactly this. Create a free account and explore it before you pay.
learn.verdictLi2: You're a hardcore coach who lives in the app and needs deep configuration: iScore is for you.
learn.verdictLi3: Your league mandates GameChanger: Keep paying it, sadly.
learn.verdictAfter: Everything else is a distant tier.
learn.faqQ1: Is Baseball Stats Tracker really $39 one-time, no catches?
learn.faqA1: Yes. One payment, lifetime access. No upsells, no premium tier, no in-app purchases. Every feature is included.
learn.faqA2: Yes — baseball and softball both, every level (Little League, travel ball, rec, high school).
learn.faqQ3: Can I import my GameChanger data?
learn.faqA3: Not currently. You'd start fresh. If this is a dealbreaker, let me know and I'll prioritize it.
learn.faqQ4: Can I try it before I buy?
learn.faqA4: Yes. Create a free account and explore the app — set up a team, add players, see the interface. Creating and scoring games requires the $39 purchase.
learn.faqQ5: Will the price ever go up?
learn.faqA5: For new buyers, possibly. For people who've already bought, never — that's the whole point of “one-time.”
learn.ctaP: $39 once. Score your first game tonight. No app store, no subscription. You just own it.

users.deleteNote: You can restore this user later from the admin panel.
